hints:
Vermeer mostly painted genre scenes, which were scenes of everyday life.

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/holland/v/vermeer-woman-with-balance)

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3f4987fc37e105c8f35f3c5d9c8bc348.jpeg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Art historians are able to agree on which paintings were painted by Rembrandt.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Rembrandt's work and style were so influential that he had students, followers and forgers create work in his style, Art historians continue to challenge the authenticity of certain works.

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1600-1700-the-baroque/holland/a/is-it-a-genuine-rembrandt)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Rembrandt was often interested in which aspect of the figures he painted?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Rembrandt often showed figures who seem to be deep in thought.

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/holland/v/rembrandt-van-rijn-bathsheba-at-her-bath-1654)

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c31e8d29ce83a47545689d0510c5ab1d.jpeg)

choices.content: their relationship to the natural or built environment

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements is true of Vermeer's *Woman Holding a Balance*?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3f4987fc37e105c8f35f3c5d9c8bc348.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The meanings of Vermeer's paintings are often unclear.The painting has themes of time and change and possibly expresses a tension between wealth and piety.

Instead of the drama that is so prevalent in the Baroque art of Italy, Vermeer stresses quiet introspection.

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/holland/v/vermeer-woman-with-balance)

choices.content: The meanings of Vermeer's paintings are often unclear.
The painting has themes of time and change.
The painting may express a tension between wealth and piety.
This painting was commissioned for a church.
Vermeer created bold transitions between colors to give the painting a sense of drama.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Seventeenth century Dutch genre painting was often bawdy and explicit in its subject matter and meaning and this is true of Vermeer's paintings.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Seventeenth century Dutch genre painting is normally bawdy and explicit in its subject matter and meaning, but this is not true of Vermeer's painting.

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/holland/v/jan-vermeer-the-glass-of-wine-c-1661)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Vermeer's *The Art of Painting* is about . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/248e20db1ef182abd7653f7b1c719574.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This painting is about the roles of art and history and the role of the artist in making history. It is also about transforming the real world into art.

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/holland/v/vermeer-the-art-of-painting)

choices.content: the roles of art and history and the role of the artist in making history.
transforming the real world into art.
a subtle portrayal of an illicit relationship.
the relationship between artist and patron.
